revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''河上邦彦'''（かわかみ くにひこ、[[1945年]][[5月10日]]<ref>『現代日本人名録』2002年</ref>- ）は、考古学者、神戸山...'
revision.text:
'''河上邦彦'''（かわかみ くにひこ、[[1945年]][[5月10日]]<ref>『現代日本人名録』2002年</ref>- ）は、考古学者、[[神戸山手大学]]教授。
大阪市生まれ。1965年[[関西大学]]大学院文学科日本史学専攻修士課程修了。95年「後期古墳の研究」で関西大学[[文学博士]]。1970年[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]勤務、72年同技師。80-82年[[北京大学]]に学ぶ。樫原考古学研究所第三調査室長、副所長および同附属博物館館長を経て、神戸山手大学教授。中国[[西北大学]]客員教授。中国社会科学院古代文明研究センター客員研究員。[[広陵町]]教育委員会文化財保存センター所長。[[飛鳥京]]跡、[[牧野古墳]]、[[東明神古墳]]、[[下池山古墳]]、[[黒塚古墳]]など多くの調査をおこなう。<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
==著書== *『考古学点描』六興出版 ロッコウブックス 1989 *『後・終末期古墳の研究』雄山閣出版 1995 *『飛鳥を掘る』講談社選書メチエ 2003 *『飛鳥発掘物語』産経新聞ニュースサービス 2004 *『大和葛城の大古墳群 [[馬見古墳群]]』新泉社 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」 2006
===共編著=== *『飛鳥学』全2巻 [[和田萃]],[[菅谷文則]]共編著 人文書院 1996 *『大和の終末期古墳』編 学生社 2005 *『新近畿日本叢書大和の考古学 第3巻 大和の古墳 2』奈良県立橿原考古学研究所監修（編）近畿日本鉄道 2006 *『[[三輪山]]と古代の神まつり』[[小笠原好彦]],菅谷文則,鈴鹿千代乃,[[平林章仁]],[[広瀬和雄]],和田萃共著 学生社 2008
==論文== *[http://ci.nii.ac.jp/search?q=%E6%B2%B3%E4%B8%8A%E9%82%A6%E5%BD%A6&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=1&type=1 Cinii]

'''河上 邦彦'''（かわかみ くにひこ、[[1945年]][[5月10日]]<ref>『現代日本人名録』2002年</ref> - ）は、考古学者、[[神戸山手大学]]教授。
大阪市生まれ。1965年[[関西大学]]大学院文学科日本史学専攻修士課程修了。95年「後期古墳の研究」で関西大学[[文学博士]]。1970年[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]勤務、72年同技師。80-82年[[北京大学]]に学ぶ。樫原考古学研究所第三調査室長、副所長および同附属博物館館長を経て、神戸山手大学教授。中国[[西北大学]]客員教授。中国社会科学院古代文明研究センター客員研究員。[[広陵町]]教育委員会文化財保存センター所長。[[飛鳥京]]跡、[[牧野古墳]]、[[東明神古墳]]、[[下池山古墳]]、[[黒塚古墳]]など多くの調査をおこなう。<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
==著書== *『考古学点描』六興出版 ロッコウブックス 1989 *『後・終末期古墳の研究』雄山閣出版 1995 *『飛鳥を掘る』講談社選書メチエ 2003 *『飛鳥発掘物語』産経新聞ニュースサービス 2004 *『大和葛城の大古墳群 [[馬見古墳群]]』新泉社 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」 2006
===共編著=== *『飛鳥学』全2巻 [[和田萃]],[[菅谷文則]]共編著 人文書院 1996 *『大和の終末期古墳』編 学生社 2005 *『新近畿日本叢書大和の考古学 第3巻 大和の古墳 2』奈良県立橿原考古学研究所監修（編）近畿日本鉄道 2006 *『[[三輪山]]と古代の神まつり』[[小笠原好彦]],菅谷文則,鈴鹿千代乃,[[平林章仁]],[[広瀬和雄]],和田萃共著 学生社 2008

{{Infobox 学者 | name = 河上 邦彦 | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}}[[大阪府]] | death_date = | death_place = | othername = |main_interests = [[考古学]] |alma_mater = [[関西大学]] |workplaces = [[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] |degree = <!--学位--> | URL = | yearsactive = | spouse = | children = }} '''河上 邦彦'''（かわかみ くにひこ、[[1945年]][[5月10日]]<ref>『現代日本人名録』2002年</ref> - ）は、[[日本]]の[[考古学者]]、元[[神戸山手大学]]教授。
==経歴== 1945年、[[大阪市]]生まれ。1965年[[関西大学]]大学院文学科日本史学専攻修士課程修了(後に1995年「後期古墳の研究」を[[関西大学]]に提出して[[文学博士]]号を取得)。
卒業後は、1970年より[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]に勤務し、1972年に同研究所技師となる。1980～82年、[[北京大学]]に留学。橿原考古学研究所第三調査室長、副所長および同附属博物館館長を務め、[[神戸山手大学]]教授を兼任した。また、中国[[西北大学]]客員教授。中国社会科学院古代文明研究センター客員研究員。[[広陵町]]教育委員会文化財保存センター所長。
[[飛鳥京]]跡、[[牧野古墳]]、[[東明神古墳]]、[[下池山古墳]]、[[黒塚古墳]]など多くの調査を担当した。<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
==著書== *『考古学点描』六興出版 ロッコウブックス 1989 *『後・終末期古墳の研究』雄山閣出版 1995 *『飛鳥を掘る』講談社選書メチエ 2003 *『飛鳥発掘物語』[[産経新聞]]ニュースサービス 2004 *『大和葛城の大古墳群 [[馬見古墳群]]』新泉社 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」 2006
===共編著=== *『飛鳥学』全2巻 [[和田萃]],[[菅谷文則]]共編著 人文書院 1996 *『大和の終末期古墳』編 学生社 2005 *『新近畿日本叢書大和の考古学 第3巻 大和の古墳 2』奈良県立橿原考古学研究所監修（編）近畿日本鉄道 2006 *『[[三輪山]]と古代の神まつり』[[小笠原好彦]],菅谷文則,鈴鹿千代乃,[[平林章仁]],[[広瀬和雄]],和田萃共著 学生社 2008
==論文== *[https://ci.nii.ac.jp/search?q=%E6%B2%B3%E4%B8%8A%E9%82%A6%E5%BD%A6&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=1&type=1 Cinii(河上邦彦)]
== 脚注 == <references />

卒業後は、1970年より[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]に勤務し、1972年に同研究所技師となる。1980～82年、[[北京大学]]に留学。橿原考古学研究所第三調査室長、副所長および同附属博物館館長を務め、[[神戸山手大学]]教授を兼任した。また、中国[[西北大学]]客員教授。中国社会科学院古代文明研究センター客員研究員。[[広陵町]]教育委員会文化財保存センター所長。
[[飛鳥京]]跡、[[牧野古墳]]、[[東明神古墳]]、[[下池山古墳]]、[[黒塚古墳]]など多くの調査を担当した。<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>

{{Infobox 学者 | name = 河上 邦彦 | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}}[[大阪府]] | death_date = | death_place = | othername = |main_interests = [[考古学]] |alma_mater = [[関西大学]] |workplaces = [[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] |degree = <!--学位--> | URL = | yearsactive = | spouse = | children = }} '''河上 邦彦'''（かわかみ くにひこ、[[1945年]][[5月10日]]<ref>『現代日本人名録』2002年</ref> - ）は、[[日本]]の[[考古学者]]、元[[神戸山手大学]]教授。
==経歴== 1945年、[[大阪市]]生まれ。1965年[[関西大学]]大学院文学科日本史学専攻[[大学院#修士課程・博士前期課程|修士課程]]修了(後に1995年「後期古墳の研究」を[[関西大学]]に提出して[[文学博士]]号を取得)。
卒業後は、1970年より[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]に勤務し、1972年に同研究所技師となる。1980～82年、[[北京大学]]に留学。橿原考古学研究所第三調査室長、副所長および同附属博物館館長を務め、[[神戸山手大学]]教授を兼任した。また、中国[[西北大学]]客員教授。[[中国社会科学院]]古代文明研究センター客員研究員。[[広陵町]]教育委員会文化財保存センター所長。
[[飛鳥京]]跡、[[牧野古墳]]、[[東明神古墳]]、[[下池山古墳]]、[[黒塚古墳]]など多くの調査を担当した。<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
==著書== *『考古学点描』六興出版 ロッコウブックス 1989 *『後・終末期古墳の研究』[[雄山閣出版]] 1995 *『飛鳥を掘る』講談社選書メチエ 2003 *『飛鳥発掘物語』[[産経新聞]]ニュースサービス 2004 *『大和葛城の大古墳群 [[馬見古墳群]]』新泉社 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」 2006
===共編著=== *『飛鳥学』全2巻 [[和田萃]],[[菅谷文則]]共編著 人文書院 1996 *『大和の終末期古墳』編 学生社 2005 *『新近畿日本叢書大和の考古学 第3巻 大和の古墳 2』奈良県立橿原考古学研究所監修（編）近畿日本鉄道 2006 *『[[三輪山]]と古代の神まつり』[[小笠原好彦]],菅谷文則,鈴鹿千代乃,[[平林章仁]],[[広瀬和雄]],和田萃共著 学生社 2008
==論文== *[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E6%B2%B3%E4%B8%8A%E9%82%A6%E5%BD%A6&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=1&type=1 Cinii(河上邦彦)]
== 脚注 == <references />

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Infobox academic | name = Kunihiko Kawakami | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Osaka Prefecture]] | death_date = | death_place = | other_names = | main_interests = [[Archaeology]] | alma_mater = [[Kansai University]] | workplaces = [[Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] | degree = Doctor of Letters | URL = | years_acti..."
revision.text:
{{Infobox academic | name = Kunihiko Kawakami | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Osaka Prefecture]] | death_date = | death_place = | other_names = | main_interests = [[Archaeology]] | alma_mater = [[Kansai University]] | workplaces = [[Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] | degree = Doctor of Letters | URL = | years_active = | spouse = | children = }} '''Kunihiko Kawakami''' (河上 邦彦, born [[May 10]], [[1945]]) is a Japanese [[archaeologist]] and former professor at [[Kobe Yamate University]].
== Career == Kunihiko Kawakami was born in [[Osaka City]] in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at [[Kansai University]] with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he was awarded a [[Doctor of Letters]] degree from Kansai University for his dissertation, *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*.
Kawakami joined the [[Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at [[Peking University]]. He held various roles, including Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at [[Northwest University (China)]], and visiting researcher at the [[Chinese Academy of Social Sciences]]' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He also directed the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the [[Kōryō Town]] Board of Education.
Kawakami was involved in numerous excavations, including those at the [[Asuka Palace]], [[Makino Kofun]], [[Tōmyōjin Kofun]], [[Shimoikeyama Kofun]], and [[Kurozuka Kofun]].
== Publications == ### Books * *Archaeological Sketches*, Rokkō Shuppan, 1989 * *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*, Yūzankaku Publishing, 1995 * *Excavating Asuka*, Kodansha Sensho Metier, 2003 * *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Sankei News Service, 2004 * *Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Tumuli*, Shinsensha, 2006
### Edited Volumes * *Studies on Asuka* (2 volumes), co-edited with [[Wada Atsushi]] and [[Sugaya Fuminori]], Jinbun Shoin, 1996 * *Terminal Kofun in Yamato*, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Archaeology of Yamato: Volume 3 - Yamato’s Kofun 2*, overseen by Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, Kinki Nippon Railway, 2006 * *Mount Miwa and Ancient Kami Worship*, co-authored with [[Ogasawara Yoshihiko]], Sugaya Fuminori, Suzuka Chiyono, Hirabayashi Akihito, Hirose Kazuo, and Wada Atsushi, Gakuseisha, 2008
== Articles == * [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E6%B2%B3%E4%B8%8A%E9%82%A6%E5%BD%A6&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=1&type=1 Cinii (Kunihiko Kawakami)]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Kunihiko Kawakami]] to [[Kawakami Kunihiko]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox academic | name = Kunihiko Kawakami | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Osaka Prefecture]] | death_date = | death_place = | other_names = | main_interests = [[Archaeology]] | alma_mater = [[Kansai University]] | workplaces = [[Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] | degree = Doctor of Letters | URL = | years_active = | spouse = | children = }} '''Kunihiko Kawakami''' (河上 邦彦, born [[May 10]], [[1945]]) is a Japanese [[archaeologist]] and former professor at [[Kobe Yamate University]].
== Career == Kunihiko Kawakami was born in [[Osaka City]] in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at [[Kansai University]] with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he was awarded a [[Doctor of Letters]] degree from Kansai University for his dissertation, *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*.
Kawakami joined the [[Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at [[Peking University]]. He held various roles, including Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at [[Northwest University (China)]], and visiting researcher at the [[Chinese Academy of Social Sciences]]' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He also directed the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the [[Kōryō Town]] Board of Education.
Kawakami was involved in numerous excavations, including those at the [[Asuka Palace]], [[Makino Kofun]], [[Tōmyōjin Kofun]], [[Shimoikeyama Kofun]], and [[Kurozuka Kofun]].
== Publications == ### Books * *Archaeological Sketches*, Rokkō Shuppan, 1989 * *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*, Yūzankaku Publishing, 1995 * *Excavating Asuka*, Kodansha Sensho Metier, 2003 * *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Sankei News Service, 2004 * *Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Tumuli*, Shinsensha, 2006
### Edited Volumes * *Studies on Asuka* (2 volumes), co-edited with [[Wada Atsushi]] and [[Sugaya Fuminori]], Jinbun Shoin, 1996 * *Terminal Kofun in Yamato*, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Archaeology of Yamato: Volume 3 - Yamato’s Kofun 2*, overseen by Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, Kinki Nippon Railway, 2006 * *Mount Miwa and Ancient Kami Worship*, co-authored with [[Ogasawara Yoshihiko]], Sugaya Fuminori, Suzuka Chiyono, Hirabayashi Akihito, Hirose Kazuo, and Wada Atsushi, Gakuseisha, 2008
== Articles == * [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E6%B2%B3%E4%B8%8A%E9%82%A6%E5%BD%A6&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=1&type=1 Cinii (Kunihiko Kawakami)]
== References == <references />

After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at {{ill|Peking University|ja|北京大学}}. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|ja|西北大学}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|ja|中国社会科学院}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō Town|ja|広陵町}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka Palace|ja|飛鳥京}}, {{ill|Makino Kofun|ja|牧野古墳}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>

== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}} in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at {{ill|Peking University|ja|北京大学}}. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|ja|西北大学}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|ja|中国社会科学院}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō Town|ja|広陵町}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka Palace|ja|飛鳥京}}, {{ill|Makino Kofun|ja|牧野古墳}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>

In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at {{ill|Peking University|ja|北京大学}}. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|ja|西北大学}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|ja|中国社会科学院}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō Town|ja|広陵町}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka Palace|ja|飛鳥京}}, {{ill|Makino Kofun|ja|牧野古墳}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
== 著書 ==
* *Archaeological Sketches*, Rokkō Shuppan, 1989 * *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*, Yūzankaku Publishing, 1995 * *Excavating Asuka*, Kodansha Sensho Metier, 2003 * *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Sankei News Service, 2004 * *Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Tumuli*, Shinsensha, 2006
=== 共編著 ===
* *Studies on Asuka* (2 volumes), co-edited with {{ill|Wada Atsushi|ja|和田萃}} and {{ill|Sugaya Fuminori|ja|菅谷文則}}, Jinbun Shoin, 1996 * *Terminal Kofun in Yamato*, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Archaeology of Yamato: Volume 3 - Yamato’s Kofun 2*, overseen by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, Kinki Nippon Railway, 2006 * *Mount Miwa and Ancient Kami Worship*, co-authored with {{ill|Ogasawara Yoshihiko|ja|小笠原好彦}}, Sugaya Fuminori, Suzuka Chiyono, Hirabayashi Akihito, Hirose Kazuo, and Wada Atsushi, Gakuseisha, 2008
== 論文 ==

{{Infobox academic | name = Kunihiko Kawakami | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Osaka Prefecture]] | death_date = | death_place = | other_names = | main_interests = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}} | alma_mater = {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} | workplaces = {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} | degree = Doctor of Letters | URL = | years_active = | spouse = | children = }} '''Kunihiko Kawakami''' (河上 邦彦, born [[May 10]], [[1945]]) is a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者}} and former professor at {{ill|Kobe Yamate University|ja|神戸山手大学}}.
== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}} in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at {{ill|Peking University|ja|北京大学}}. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|ja|西北大学}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|ja|中国社会科学院}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō Town|ja|広陵町}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka Palace|ja|飛鳥京}}, {{ill|Makino Kofun|ja|牧野古墳}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
== 著書 ==
* *Archaeological Sketches*, Rokkō Shuppan, 1989 * *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*, Yūzankaku Publishing, 1995 * *Excavating Asuka*, Kodansha Sensho Metier, 2003 * *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Sankei News Service, 2004 * *Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Tumuli*, Shinsensha, 2006
=== 共編著 ===
* *Studies on Asuka* (2 volumes), co-edited with {{ill|Wada Atsushi|ja|和田萃}} and {{ill|Sugaya Fuminori|ja|菅谷文則}}, Jinbun Shoin, 1996 * *Terminal Kofun in Yamato*, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Archaeology of Yamato: Volume 3 - Yamato’s Kofun 2*, overseen by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, Kinki Nippon Railway, 2006 * *Mount Miwa and Ancient Kami Worship*, co-authored with {{ill|Ogasawara Yoshihiko|ja|小笠原好彦}}, Sugaya Fuminori, Suzuka Chiyono, Hirabayashi Akihito, Hirose Kazuo, and Wada Atsushi, Gakuseisha, 2008
== 論文 ==
* [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E6%B2%B3%E4%B8%8A%E9%82%A6%E5%BD%A6&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=1&type=1 Cinii (Kunihiko Kawakami)]

In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at {{ill|Peking University|ja|北京大学}}. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|ja|西北大学}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|ja|中国社会科学院}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō Town|ja|広陵町}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka Palace|ja|飛鳥京}}, {{ill|Makino Kofun|ja|牧野古墳}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>

== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}} in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at {{ill|Peking University|ja|北京大学}}. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|ja|西北大学}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|ja|中国社会科学院}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō Town|ja|広陵町}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka Palace|ja|飛鳥京}}, {{ill|Makino Kofun|ja|牧野古墳}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
== 著書 ==
* *Archaeological Sketches*, Rokkō Shuppan, 1989 * *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*, Yūzankaku Publishing, 1995 * *Excavating Asuka*, Kodansha Sensho Metier, 2003 * *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Sankei News Service, 2004 * *Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Tumuli*, Shinsensha, 2006
=== 共編著 ===
* *Studies on Asuka* (2 volumes), co-edited with {{ill|Wada Atsushi|ja|和田萃}} and {{ill|Sugaya Fuminori|ja|菅谷文則}}, Jinbun Shoin, 1996 * *Terminal Kofun in Yamato*, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Archaeology of Yamato: Volume 3 - Yamato’s Kofun 2*, overseen by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, Kinki Nippon Railway, 2006 * *Mount Miwa and Ancient Kami Worship*, co-authored with {{ill|Ogasawara Yoshihiko|ja|小笠原好彦}}, Sugaya Fuminori, Suzuka Chiyono, Hirabayashi Akihito, Hirose Kazuo, and Wada Atsushi, Gakuseisha, 2008
== 論文 ==

revision.text:
{{Infobox academic | name = Kunihiko Kawakami | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Osaka Prefecture]] | death_date = | death_place = | other_names = | main_interests = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}} | alma_mater = {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} | workplaces = {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} | degree = Doctor of Letters | URL = | years_active = | spouse = | children = }} '''Kunihiko Kawakami''' (河上 邦彦, born [[May 10]], [[1945]]) is a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者}} and former professor at {{ill|Kobe Yamate University|ja|神戸山手大学}}.
== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}} in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at {{ill|Peking University|ja|北京大学}}. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|ja|西北大学}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|ja|中国社会科学院}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō Town|ja|広陵町}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka Palace|ja|飛鳥京}}, {{ill|Makino Kofun|ja|牧野古墳}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
== 著書 ==
* *Archaeological Sketches*, Rokkō Shuppan, 1989 * *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*, Yūzankaku Publishing, 1995 * *Excavating Asuka*, Kodansha Sensho Metier, 2003 * *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Sankei News Service, 2004 * *Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Tumuli*, Shinsensha, 2006
=== 共編著 ===
* *Studies on Asuka* (2 volumes), co-edited with {{ill|Wada Atsushi|ja|和田萃}} and {{ill|Sugaya Fuminori|ja|菅谷文則}}, Jinbun Shoin, 1996 * *Terminal Kofun in Yamato*, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Archaeology of Yamato: Volume 3 - Yamato’s Kofun 2*, overseen by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, Kinki Nippon Railway, 2006 * *Mount Miwa and Ancient Kami Worship*, co-authored with {{ill|Ogasawara Yoshihiko|ja|小笠原好彦}}, Sugaya Fuminori, Suzuka Chiyono, Hirabayashi Akihito, Hirose Kazuo, and Wada Atsushi, Gakuseisha, 2008
== 論文 ==
* [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E6%B2%B3%E4%B8%8A%E9%82%A6%E5%BD%A6&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=1&type=1 Cinii (Kunihiko Kawakami)]
== 脚注 ==

revision.text:
{{Infobox academic | name = Kunihiko Kawakami | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Osaka Prefecture]] | death_date = | death_place = | other_names = | main_interests = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}} | alma_mater = {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} | workplaces = {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} | degree = Doctor of Letters | URL = | years_active = | spouse = | children = }} '''Kunihiko Kawakami''' (河上 邦彦, born [[May 10]], [[1945]]) is a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者}} and former professor at {{ill|Kobe Yamate University|ja|神戸山手大学}}.
== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}} in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at {{ill|Peking University|ja|北京大学}}. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|ja|西北大学}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|ja|中国社会科学院}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō Town|ja|広陵町}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka Palace|ja|飛鳥京}}, {{ill|Makino Kofun|ja|牧野古墳}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
== 著書 ==
* *Archaeological Sketches*, Rokkō Shuppan, 1989 * *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*, Yūzankaku Publishing, 1995 * *Excavating Asuka*, Kodansha Sensho Metier, 2003 * *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Sankei News Service, 2004 * *Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Tumuli*, Shinsensha, 2006
=== 共編著 ===
* *Studies on Asuka* (2 volumes), co-edited with {{ill|Wada Atsushi|ja|和田萃}} and {{ill|Sugaya Fuminori|ja|菅谷文則}}, Jinbun Shoin, 1996 * *Terminal Kofun in Yamato*, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Archaeology of Yamato: Volume 3 - Yamato’s Kofun 2*, overseen by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, Kinki Nippon Railway, 2006 * *Mount Miwa and Ancient Kami Worship*, co-authored with {{ill|Ogasawara Yoshihiko|ja|小笠原好彦}}, Sugaya Fuminori, Suzuka Chiyono, Hirabayashi Akihito, Hirose Kazuo, and Wada Atsushi, Gakuseisha, 2008
== 論文 ==
* [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E6%B2%B3%E4%B8%8A%E9%82%A6%E5%BD%A6&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=1&type=1 Cinii (Kunihiko Kawakami)]

Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|ja|西北大学}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|ja|中国社会科学院}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō Town|ja|広陵町}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka Palace|ja|飛鳥京}}, {{ill|Makino Kofun|ja|牧野古墳}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
== 著書 ==

=== 共編著 ===

revision.text:
{{Infobox academic | name = Kunihiko Kawakami | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Osaka Prefecture]] | death_date = | death_place = | other_names = | main_interests = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}} | alma_mater = {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} | workplaces = {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} | degree = Doctor of Letters | URL = | years_active = | spouse = | children = }} '''Kunihiko Kawakami''' (河上 邦彦, born [[May 10]], [[1945]]) is a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者}} and former professor at {{ill|Kobe Yamate University|ja|神戸山手大学}}.
== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}} in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at {{ill|Peking University|ja|北京大学}}. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|ja|西北大学}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|ja|中国社会科学院}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō Town|ja|広陵町}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka Palace|ja|飛鳥京}}, {{ill|Makino Kofun|ja|牧野古墳}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
== 著書 ==
* *Archaeological Sketches*, Rokkō Shuqqan, 1989 * *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*, Yūzankaku Publishing, 1995 * *Excavating Asuka*, Kodansha Sensho Metier, 2003 * *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Sankei News Service, 2004 * *Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Tumuli*, Shinsensha, 2006
=== 共編著 ===
* *Studies on Asuka* (2 volumes), co-edited with {{ill|Wada Atsushi|ja|和田萃}} and {{ill|Sugaya Fuminori|ja|菅谷文則}}, Jinbun Shoin, 1996 * *Terminal Kofun in Yamato*, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Archaeology of Yamato: Volume 3 - Yamato’s Kofun 2*, overseen by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, Kinki Nippon Railway, 2006 * *Mount Miwa and Ancient Kami Worship*, co-authored with {{ill|Ogasawara Yoshihiko|ja|小笠原好彦}}, Sugaya Fuminori, Suzuka Chiyono, Hirabayashi Akihito, Hirose Kazuo, and Wada Atsushi, Gakuseisha, 2008
== 論文 ==
* [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E6%B2%B3%E4%B8%8A%E9%82%A6%E5%BD%A6&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=1&type=1 Cinii (Kunihiko Kawakami)]

revision.text:
{{Infobox academic | name = Kunihiko Kawakami | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Osaka Prefecture]] | death_date = | death_place = | other_names = | main_interests = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}} | alma_mater = {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} | workplaces = {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} | degree = Doctor of Letters | URL = | years_active = | spouse = | children = }} '''Kunihiko Kawakami''' (河上 邦彦, born [[May 10]], [[1945]]) is a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者}} and former professor at {{ill|Kobe Yamate University|ja|神戸山手大学}}.
== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}} in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at {{ill|Peking University|ja|北京大学}}. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|ja|西北大学}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|ja|中国社会科学院}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō Town|ja|広陵町}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka Palace|ja|飛鳥京}}, {{ill|Makino Kofun|ja|牧野古墳}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
== 著書 ==
* *Archaeological Sketches*, Rokkō Shuqqan, 1989 * *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*, Yūzankaku Publishing, 1995 * *Excavating Asuka*, Kodansha Sensho Metier, 2003 * *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Sankei News Service, 2004 * *Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Tumuli*, Shinsensha, 2006
=== 共編著 ===
* *Studies on Asuka* (2 volumes), co-edited with {{ill|Wada Atsushi|ja|和田萃}} and {{ill|Sugaya Fuminori|ja|菅谷文則}}, Jinbun Shoin, 1996 * *Terminal Kofun in Yamato*, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Archaeology of Yamato: Volume 3 - Yamato’s Kofun 2*, overseen by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, Kinki Nippon Railway, 2006 * *Mount Miwa and Ancient Kami Worship*, co-authored with {{ill|Ogasawara Yoshihiko|ja|小笠原好彦}}, Sugaya Fuminori, Suzuka Chiyono, Hirabayashi Akihito, Hirose Kazuo, and Wada Atsushi, Gakuseisha, 2008
== 論文 ==
* [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E6%B2%B3%E4%B8%8A%E9%82%A6%E5%BD%A6&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=1&type=1 Cinii (Kunihiko Kawakami)]

{{Infobox academic | name = Kunihiko Kawakami | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Osaka Prefecture]] | death_date = | death_place = | other_names = | main_interests = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}} | alma_mater = {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} | workplaces = {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} | degree = Doctor of Letters | URL = | years_active = | spouse = | children = }} '''Kunihiko Kawakami''' (河上 邦彦, born [[May 10]], [[1945]]) is a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者}} and former professor at {{ill|Kobe Yamate University|ja|神戸山手大学}}.
== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}} in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at {{ill|Peking University|ja|北京大学}}. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|ja|西北大学}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|ja|中国社会科学院}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō Town|ja|広陵町}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka Palace|ja|飛鳥京}}, {{ill|Makino Kofun|ja|牧野古墳}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
== 著書 ==
* *Archaeological Sketches*, Rokkō Shuqqan, 1989 * *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*, Yūzankaku Publishing, 1995 * *Excavating Asuka*, Kodansha Sensho Metier, 2003 * *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Sankei News Service, 2004 * *Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Tumuli*, Shinsensha, 2006
=== 共編著 ===

== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}} in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at {{ill|Peking University|ja|北京大学}}. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|ja|西北大学}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|ja|中国社会科学院}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. {{Infobox academic | name = Kunihiko Kawakami | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Osaka Prefecture]] | death_date = | death_place = | other_names = | main_interests = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}} | alma_mater = {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} | workplaces = {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} | degree = Doctor of Letters | URL = | years_active = | spouse = | children = }} '''Kunihiko Kawakami''' (河上 邦彦, born [[May 10]], [[1945]]) is a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者}} and former professor at {{ill|Kobe Yamate University|ja|神戸山手大学}}.
== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}} in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at {{ill|Peking University|ja|北京大学}}. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|ja|西北大学}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|ja|中国社会科学院}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō Town|ja|広陵町}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka Palace|ja|飛鳥京}}, {{ill|Makino Kofun|ja|牧野古墳}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
== 著書 ==
* *Archaeological Sketches*, Rokkō Shu11an, 1989 * *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*, Yūzankaku Publishing, 1995 * *Excavating Asuka*, Kodansha Sensho Metier, 2003 * *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Sankei News Service, 2004 * *Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Tumuli*, Shinsensha, 2006
=== 共編著 ===
* *Studies on Asuka* (2 volumes), co-edited with {{ill|Wada Atsushi|ja|和田萃}} and {{ill|Sugaya Fuminori|ja|菅谷文則}}, Jinbun Shoin, 1996 * *Terminal Kofun in Yamato*, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Archaeology of Yamato: Volume 3 - Yamato’s Kofun 2*, overseen by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, Kinki Nippon Railway, 2006 * *Mount Miwa and Ancient Kami Worship*, co-authored with {{ill|Ogasawara Yoshihiko|ja|小笠原好彦}}, Sugaya Fuminori, Suzuka Chiyono, Hirabayashi Akihito, Hirose Kazuo, and Wada Atsushi, Gakuseisha, 2008

revision.text:
{{Infobox academic | name = Kunihiko Kawakami | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Osaka Prefecture]] | death_date = | death_place = | other_names = | main_interests = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}} | alma_mater = {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} | workplaces = {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} | degree = Doctor of Letters | URL = | years_active = | spouse = | children = }} '''Kunihiko Kawakami''' (河上 邦彦, born [[May 10]], [[1945]]) is a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者}} and former professor at {{ill|Kobe Yamate University|ja|神戸山手大学}}.
== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}} in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at {{ill|Peking University|ja|北京大学}}. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|ja|西北大学}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|ja|中国社会科学院}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō Town|ja|広陵町}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka Palace|ja|飛鳥京}}, {{ill|Makino Kofun|ja|牧野古墳}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
== 著書 ==
* *Archaeological Sketches*, Rokkō Shu11an, 1989 * *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*, Yūzankaku Publishing, 1995 * *Excavating Asuka*, Kodansha Sensho Metier, 2003 * *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Sankei News Service, 2004 * *Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Tumuli*, Shinsensha, 2006
=== 共編著 ===
* *Studies on Asuka* (2 volumes), co-edited with {{ill|Wada Atsushi|ja|和田萃}} and {{ill|Sugaya Fuminori|ja|菅谷文則}}, Jinbun Shoin, 1996 * *Terminal Kofun in Yamato*, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Archaeology of Yamato: Volume 3 - Yamato’s Kofun 2*, overseen by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, Kinki Nippon Railway, 2006 * *Mount Miwa and Ancient Kami Worship*, co-authored with {{ill|Ogasawara Yoshihiko|ja|小笠原好彦}}, Sugaya Fuminori, Suzuka Chiyono, Hirabayashi Akihito, Hirose Kazuo, and Wada Atsushi, Gakuseisha, 2008
== 論文 ==
* [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E6%B2%B3%E4%B8%8A%E9%82%A6%E5%BD%A6&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=1&type=1 Cinii (Kunihiko Kawakami)]

{{Infobox academic | name = Kunihiko Kawakami | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Osaka Prefecture]] | death_date = | death_place = | other_names = | main_interests = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}} | alma_mater = {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} | workplaces = {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} | degree = Doctor of Letters | URL = | years_active = | spouse = | children = }} '''Kunihiko Kawakami''' (河上 邦彦, born [[May 10]], [[1945]]) is a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者}} and former professor at {{ill|Kobe Yamate University|ja|神戸山手大学}}.
== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}} in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at {{ill|Peking University|ja|北京大学}}. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|ja|西北大学}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|ja|中国社会科学院}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō Town|ja|広陵町}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka Palace|ja|飛鳥京}}, {{ill|Makino Kofun|ja|牧野古墳}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
== 著書 ==
* *Archaeological Sketches*, Rokkō Shu11an, 1989 * *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*, Yūzankaku Publishing, 1995 * *Excavating Asuka*, Kodansha Sensho Metier, 2003 * *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Sankei News Service, 2004 * *Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Tumuli*, Shinsensha, 2006
=== 共編著 ===
* *Studies on Asuka* (2 volumes), co-edited with {{ill|Wada Atsushi|ja|和田萃}} and {{ill|Sugaya Fuminori|ja|菅谷文則}}, Jinbun Shoin, 1996 * *Terminal Kofun in Yamato*, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Archaeology of Yamato: Volume 3 - Yamato’s Kofun 2*, overseen by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, Kinki Nippon Railway, 2006 * *Mount Miwa and Ancient Kami Worship*, co-authored with {{ill|Ogasawara Yoshihiko|ja|小笠原好彦}}, Sugaya Fuminori, Suzuka Chiyono, Hirabayashi Akihito, Hirose Kazuo, and Wada Atsushi, Gakuseisha, 2008
== 論文 ==

revision.text:
{{Infobox academic | name = Kunihiko Kawakami | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Osaka Prefecture]] | death_date = | death_place = | other_names = | main_interests = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学|12=draft}} | alma_mater = {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学|12=draft}} | workplaces = {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} | degree = Doctor of Letters | URL = | years_active = | spouse = | children = }} '''Kunihiko Kawakami''' (河上 邦彦, born [[May 10]], [[1945]]) is a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|12=draft}} and former professor at {{ill|Kobe Yamate University|ja|神戸山手大学|12=draft}}.
== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市|12=draft}} in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学|12=draft}} with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士|12=draft}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at {{ill|Peking University|ja|北京大学|12=draft}}. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|ja|西北大学|12=draft}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|ja|中国社会科学院|12=draft}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō Town|ja|広陵町|12=draft}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka Palace|ja|飛鳥京|12=draft}}, {{ill|Makino Kofun|ja|牧野古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳|12=draft}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
== 著書 ==
* *Archaeological Sketches*, Rokkō Shu11an, 1989 * *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*, Yūzankaku Publishing, 1995 * *Excavating Asuka*, Kodansha Sensho Metier, 2003 * *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Sankei News Service, 2004 * *Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Tumuli*, Shinsensha, 2006
=== 共編著 ===

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox academic | name = Kunihiko Kawakami | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Osaka Prefecture]] | death_date = | death_place = | other_names = | main_interests = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学|12=draft}} | alma_mater = {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学|12=draft}} | workplaces = {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} | degree = Doctor of Letters | URL = | years_active = | spouse = | children = }} '''Kunihiko Kawakami''' (河上 邦彦, born [[May 10]], [[1945]]) is a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|12=draft}} and former professor at {{ill|Kobe Yamate University|ja|神戸山手大学|12=draft}}.
== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市|12=draft}} in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学|12=draft}} with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士|12=draft}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at {{ill|Peking University|ja|北京大学|12=draft}}. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|ja|西北大学|12=draft}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|ja|中国社会科学院|12=draft}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō Town|ja|広陵町|12=draft}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka Palace|ja|飛鳥京|12=draft}}, {{ill|Makino Kofun|ja|牧野古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳|12=draft}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
== 著書 ==
* *Archaeological Sketches*, Rokkō Shu11an, 1989 * *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*, Yūzankaku Publishing, 1995 * *Excavating Asuka*, Kodansha Sensho Metier, 2003 * *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Sankei News Service, 2004 * *Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Tumuli*, Shinsensha, 2006
=== 共編著 ===
* *Studies on Asuka* (2 volumes), co-edited with {{ill|Wada Atsushi|ja|和田萃|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sugaya Fuminori|ja|菅谷文則|12=draft}}, Jinbun Shoin, 1996 * *Terminal Kofun in Yamato*, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Archaeology of Yamato: Volume 3 - Yamato’s Kofun 2*, overseen by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, Kinki Nippon Railway, 2006 * *Mount Miwa and Ancient Kami Worship*, co-authored with {{ill|Ogasawara Yoshihiko|ja|小笠原好彦|12=draft}}, Sugaya Fuminori, Suzuka Chiyono, Hirabayashi Akihito, Hirose Kazuo, and Wada Atsushi, Gakuseisha, 2008
== 論文 ==
* [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E6%B2%B3%E4%B8%8A%E9%82%A6%E5%BD%A6&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=1&type=1 Cinii (Kunihiko Kawakami)]

revision.comment: 18 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:河上邦彦 up to rev 98692271
revision.text:
{{Infobox 学者 | name = 河上 邦彦 | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}}[[大阪府]] | death_date = | death_place = | othername = |main_interests = [[考古学]] |alma_mater = [[関西大学]] |workplaces = [[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] |degree = <!--学位--> | URL = | yearsactive = | spouse = | children = }} '''河上 邦彦'''（かわかみ くにひこ、[[1945年]][[5月10日]]<ref>『現代日本人名録』2002年</ref> - ）は、[[日本]]の[[考古学者]]、元[[神戸山手大学]]教授。
==経歴== 1945年、[[大阪市]]生まれ。1965年[[関西大学]]大学院文学科日本史学専攻[[大学院#修士課程・博士前期課程|修士課程]]修了(後に1995年「後期古墳の研究」を[[関西大学]]に提出して[[文学博士]]の学位を取得)。
卒業後は、1970年より[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]に勤務し、1972年に同研究所技師となる。1980～82年、[[北京大学]]に留学。橿原考古学研究所第三調査室長、副所長および同附属博物館館長を務め、[[神戸山手大学]]教授を兼任した。また、中国[[西北大学]]客員教授。[[中国社会科学院]]古代文明研究センター客員研究員。[[広陵町]]教育委員会文化財保存センター所長。
[[飛鳥京]]跡、[[牧野古墳]]、[[東明神古墳]]、[[下池山古墳]]、[[黒塚古墳]]など多くの調査を担当した<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>。
==著書== *『考古学点描』六興出版 ロッコウブックス 1989 *『後・終末期古墳の研究』[[雄山閣出版]] 1995 *『飛鳥を掘る』講談社選書メチエ 2003 *『飛鳥発掘物語』[[産経新聞]]ニュースサービス 2004 *『大和葛城の大古墳群 [[馬見古墳群]]』新泉社 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」 2006
===共編著=== *『飛鳥学』全2巻 [[和田萃]],[[菅谷文則]]共編著 人文書院 1996 *『大和の終末期古墳』編 学生社 2005 *『新近畿日本叢書大和の考古学 第3巻 大和の古墳 2』奈良県立橿原考古学研究所監修（編）近畿日本鉄道 2006 *『[[三輪山]]と古代の神まつり』[[小笠原好彦]],菅谷文則,鈴鹿千代乃,[[平林章仁]],[[広瀬和雄]],和田萃共著 学生社 2008
==論文== *[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E6%B2%B3%E4%B8%8A%E9%82%A6%E5%BD%A6&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=1&type=1 Cinii(河上邦彦)]
== 脚注 == <references />
{{Normdaten}}

{{Infobox 学者 | name = 河上 邦彦 | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}}[[大阪府]] | death_date = | death_place = | othername = |main_interests = [[考古学]] |alma_mater = [[関西大学]] |workplaces = [[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] |degree = <!--学位--> | URL = | yearsactive = | spouse = | children = }} '''河上 邦彦'''（かわかみ くにひこ、[[1945年]][[5月10日]]<ref>『現代日本人名録』2002年</ref> - ）は、[[日本]]の[[考古学者]]、元[[神戸山手大学]]教授。
==経歴== 1945年、[[大阪市]]生まれ。1965年[[関西大学]]大学院文学科日本史学専攻[[大学院#修士課程・博士前期課程|修士課程]]修了(後に1995年「後期古墳の研究」を[[関西大学]]に提出して[[文学博士]]の学位を取得)。
卒業後は、1970年より[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]に勤務し、1972年に同研究所技師となる。1980～82年、[[北京大学]]に留学。橿原考古学研究所第三調査室長、副所長および同附属博物館館長を務め、[[神戸山手大学]]教授を兼任した。また、中国[[西北大学]]客員教授。[[中国社会科学院]]古代文明研究センター客員研究員。[[広陵町]]教育委員会文化財保存センター所長。
[[飛鳥京]]跡、[[牧野古墳]]、[[東明神古墳]]、[[下池山古墳]]、[[黒塚古墳]]など多くの調査を担当した<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>。
==著書== *『考古学点描』六興出版 ロッコウブックス 1989 *『後・終末期古墳の研究』[[雄山閣出版]] 1995 *『飛鳥を掘る』講談社選書メチエ 2003 *『飛鳥発掘物語』[[産経新聞]]ニュースサービス 2004 *『大和葛城の大古墳群 [[馬見古墳群]]』新泉社 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」 2006
===共編著=== *『飛鳥学』全2巻 [[和田萃]],[[菅谷文則]]共編著 人文書院 1996 *『大和の終末期古墳』編 学生社 2005 *『新近畿日本叢書大和の考古学 第3巻 大和の古墳 2』奈良県立橿原考古学研究所監修（編）近畿日本鉄道 2006 *『[[三輪山]]と古代の神まつり』[[小笠原好彦]],菅谷文則,鈴鹿千代乃,[[平林章仁]],[[広瀬和雄]],和田萃共著 学生社 2008
==論文== *[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E6%B2%B3%E4%B8%8A%E9%82%A6%E5%BD%A6&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=1&type=1 Cinii(河上邦彦)]
== 脚注 == <references />
{{Normdaten}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox academic | name = Kunihiko Kawakami | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Osaka Prefecture]] | death_date = | death_place = | other_names = | main_interests = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}} | alma_mater = {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} | workplaces = {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} | degree = Doctor of Letters | URL = | years_active = | spouse = | children = }} '''Kunihiko Kawakami''' (河上 邦彦, born [[May 10]], [[1945]]) is a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者}} and former professor at {{ill|Kobe Yamate University|ja|神戸山手大学}}.
== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}} in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学}} with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at {{ill|Peking University|ja|北京大学}}. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|ja|西北大学}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|ja|中国社会科学院}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō Town|ja|広陵町}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka Palace|ja|飛鳥京}}, {{ill|Makino Kofun|ja|牧野古墳}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
== 著書 ==
* *Archaeological Sketches*, Rokkō Shu11an, 1989 * *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*, Yūzankaku Publishing, 1995 * *Excavating Asuka*, Kodansha Sensho Metier, 2003 * *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Sankei News Service, 2004 * *Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Tumuli*, Shinsensha, 2006
=== 共編著 ===
* *Studies on Asuka* (2 volumes), co-edited with {{ill|Wada Atsushi|ja|和田萃}} and {{ill|Sugaya Fuminori|ja|菅谷文則}}, Jinbun Shoin, 1996 * *Terminal Kofun in Yamato*, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Archaeology of Yamato: Volume 3 - Yamato’s Kofun 2*, overseen by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, Kinki Nippon Railway, 2006 * *Mount Miwa and Ancient Kami Worship*, co-authored with {{ill|Ogasawara Yoshihiko|ja|小笠原好彦}}, Sugaya Fuminori, Suzuka Chiyono, Hirabayashi Akihito, Hirose Kazuo, and Wada Atsushi, Gakuseisha, 2008
== 論文 ==
* [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E6%B2%B3%E4%B8%8A%E9%82%A6%E5%BD%A6&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=1&type=1 Cinii (Kunihiko Kawakami)]

{{Infobox academic | name = Kunihiko Kawakami | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Osaka Prefecture]] | death_date = | death_place = | other_names = | main_interests = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archaeology|qq=}} | alma_mater = {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai University|qq=}} | workplaces = {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|qq=}} | degree = Doctor of Letters | URL = | years_active = | spouse = | children = }} '''Kunihiko Kawakami''' (河上 邦彦, born [[May 10]], [[1945]]) is a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|qq=}} and former professor at {{ill|Kobe Yamate University|ja|神戸山手大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Yamate University|qq=}}.
== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City|qq=}} in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai University|qq=}} with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctor of Letters|qq=}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|qq=}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at {{ill|Peking University|ja|北京大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peking University|qq=}}. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|ja|西北大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Northwest University (China)|qq=}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|ja|中国社会科学院|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|qq=}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō Town|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town|qq=}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka Palace|ja|飛鳥京|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Palace|qq=}}, {{ill|Makino Kofun|ja|牧野古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makino Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmyōjin Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurozuka Kofun|qq=}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
== 著書 ==
* *Archaeological Sketches*, Rokkō Shu11an, 1989 * *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*, Yūzankaku Publishing, 1995 * *Excavating Asuka*, Kodansha Sensho Metier, 2003 * *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Sankei News Service, 2004 * *Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Tumuli*, Shinsensha, 2006
=== 共編著 ===
* *Studies on Asuka* (2 volumes), co-edited with {{ill|Wada Atsushi|ja|和田萃|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Atsushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Sugaya Fuminori|ja|菅谷文則|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugaya Fuminori|qq=}}, Jinbun Shoin, 1996 * *Terminal Kofun in Yamato*, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Archaeology of Yamato: Volume 3 - Yamato’s Kofun 2*, overseen by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, Kinki Nippon Railway, 2006 * *Mount Miwa and Ancient Kami Worship*, co-authored with {{ill|Ogasawara Yoshihiko|ja|小笠原好彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Yoshihiko|qq=}}, Sugaya Fuminori, Suzuka Chiyono, Hirabayashi Akihito, Hirose Kazuo, and Wada Atsushi, Gakuseisha, 2008
== 論文 ==
* [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E6%B2%B3%E4%B8%8A%E9%82%A6%E5%BD%A6&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=1&type=1 Cinii (Kunihiko Kawakami)]
== References ==

He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka Palace|ja|飛鳥京|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Palace|qq=}}, {{ill|Makino Kofun|ja|牧野古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makino Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmyōjin Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurozuka Kofun|qq=}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>

{{Infobox academic | name = Kunihiko Kawakami | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Osaka Prefecture]] | death_date = | death_place = | other_names = | main_interests = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archaeology|qq=}} | alma_mater = {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai University|qq=}} | workplaces = {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|10=ja|11=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|comment=trying to get the bot to work with these using this|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|qq=}} | degree = Doctor of Letters | URL = | years_active = | spouse = | children = }} '''Kunihiko Kawakami''' (河上 邦彦, born [[May 10]], [[1945]]) is a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|qq=}} and former professor at {{ill|Kobe Yamate University|ja|神戸山手大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Yamate University|qq=}}.
== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City|qq=}} in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at {{ill|Kansai University|ja|関西大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai University|qq=}} with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctor of Letters|qq=}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|10=ja|11=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|comment=trying to get the bot to work with these using this|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|qq=}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at {{ill|Peking University|ja|北京大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peking University|qq=}}. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|ja|西北大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Northwest University (China)|qq=}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|ja|中国社会科学院|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|qq=}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō Town|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town|qq=}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka Palace|ja|飛鳥京|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Palace|qq=}}, {{ill|Makino Kofun|ja|牧野古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makino Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmyōjin Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurozuka Kofun|qq=}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>

In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctor of Letters|qq=}} degree.

revision.text:
{{Infobox academic | name = Kunihiko Kawakami | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Osaka Prefecture]] | death_date = | death_place = | other_names = | main_interests = [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]] | alma_mater = [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] | workplaces = {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|10=ja|11=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|comment=trying to get the bot to work with these using this|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|qq=}} | degree = Doctor of Letters | URL = | years_active = | spouse = | children = }} '''Kunihiko Kawakami''' (河上 邦彦, born [[May 10]], [[1945]]) is a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|qq=}} and former professor at [[:en:Kobe Yamate University|Kobe Yamate University]].
== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]] in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctor of Letters|qq=}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|10=ja|11=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|comment=trying to get the bot to work with these using this|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|qq=}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at [[:en:Peking University|Peking University]]. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at [[:en:Northwest University (China)|Northwest University (China)]], and visiting researcher at the [[:en:Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences]]' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō Town]] Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of [[:en:Asuka, Yamato|Asuka Palace]], [[:en:Bakuya Kofun|Makino Kofun]], {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmyōjin Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurozuka Kofun]].<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
== 著書 ==
* *Archaeological Sketches*, Rokkō Shu11an, 1989 * *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*, Yūzankaku Publishing, 1995 * *Excavating Asuka*, Kodansha Sensho Metier, 2003 * *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Sankei News Service, 2004 * *Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Tumuli*, Shinsensha, 2006

{{Infobox academic | name = Kunihiko Kawakami | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Osaka Prefecture]] | death_date = | death_place = | other_names = | main_interests = [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]] | alma_mater = [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] | workplaces = {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|10=ja|11=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|comment=trying to get the bot to work with these using this|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|qq=}} | degree = Doctor of Letters | URL = | years_active = | spouse = | children = }} '''Kunihiko Kawakami''' (河上 邦彦, born [[May 10]], [[1945]]) is a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|qq=}} and former professor at [[:en:Kobe Yamate University|Kobe Yamate University]].
== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]] in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctor of Letters|qq=}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|10=ja|11=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|comment=trying to get the bot to work with these using this|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|qq=}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at [[:en:Peking University|Peking University]]. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at [[:en:Northwest University (China)|Northwest University (China)]], and visiting researcher at the [[:en:Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences]]' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō Town]] Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of [[:en:Asuka, Yamato|Asuka Palace]], [[:en:Bakuya Kofun|Makino Kofun]], {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmyōjin Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurozuka Kofun]].<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>

Kunihiko Kawakami was born in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]] in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctor of Letters|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士（文学）}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at [[:en:Peking University|Peking University]]. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at [[:en:Northwest University (China)|Northwest University (China)]], and visiting researcher at the [[:en:Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences]]' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō Town]] Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of [[:en:Asuka, Yamato|Asuka Palace]], [[:en:Bakuya Kofun|Makino Kofun]], {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmyōjin Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurozuka Kofun]].<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
== 著書 ==
* *Archaeological Sketches*, Rokkō Shu11an, 1989 * *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*, Yūzankaku Publishing, 1995 * *Excavating Asuka*, Kodansha Sensho Metier, 2003 * *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Sankei News Service, 2004 * *Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Tumuli*, Shinsensha, 2006
=== 共編著 ===
* *Studies on Asuka* (2 volumes), co-edited with {{ill|Wada Atsushi|ja|和田萃|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Atsushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Sugaya Fuminori|ja|菅谷文則|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugaya Fuminori|qq=}}, Jinbun Shoin, 1996 * *Terminal Kofun in Yamato*, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Archaeology of Yamato: Volume 3 - Yamato’s Kofun 2*, overseen by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, Kinki Nippon Railway, 2006 * *Mount Miwa and Ancient Kami Worship*, co-authored with {{ill|Ogasawara Yoshihiko|ja|小笠原好彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Yoshihiko|qq=}}, Sugaya Fuminori, Suzuka Chiyono, Hirabayashi Akihito, Hirose Kazuo, and Wada Atsushi, Gakuseisha, 2008
== 論文 ==
