revision.comment: Merge [[Category:日本の博物館職員]] into [[Category:Japanese Museum Staff]] via bot
revision.text:
{{Infobox academic | name = Kunihiko Kawakami | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Osaka Prefecture]] | death_date = | death_place = | other_names = | main_interests = [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]] | alma_mater = [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] | workplaces = [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] | degree = Doctor of Letters | URL = | years_active = | spouse = | children = }} '''Kunihiko Kawakami''' (河上 邦彦, born [[:en:May 10|May 10]], [[:en:1945|1945]]) is a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 考古学}} and former professor at [[:en:Kobe Yamate University|Kobe Yamate University]].
== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]] in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctor of Letters|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士（文学）}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at [[:en:Peking University|Peking University]]. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at [[:en:Northwest University (China)|Northwest University (China)]], and visiting researcher at the [[:en:Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences]]' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō Town]] Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of [[:en:Asuka, Yamato|Asuka Palace]], [[:en:Bakuya Kofun|Makino Kofun]], {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmyōjin Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurozuka Kofun]].<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
== 著書 ==
* *Archaeological Sketches*, Rokkō Shu11an, 1989 * *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*, Yūzankaku Publishing, 1995 * *Excavating Asuka*, Kodansha Sensho Metier, 2003 * *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Sankei News Service, 2004 * *Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Tumuli*, Shinsensha, 2006
=== 共編著 ===
* *Studies on Asuka* (2 volumes), co-edited with {{ill|Wada Atsushi|ja|和田萃|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Atsushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Sugaya Fuminori|ja|菅谷文則|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugaya Fuminori|qq=}}, Jinbun Shoin, 1996 * *Terminal Kofun in Yamato*, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Archaeology of Yamato: Volume 3 - Yamato’s Kofun 2*, overseen by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, Kinki Nippon Railway, 2006 * *Mount Miwa and Ancient Kami Worship*, co-authored with {{ill|Ogasawara Yoshihiko|ja|小笠原好彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Yoshihiko|qq=}}, Sugaya Fuminori, Suzuka Chiyono, Hirabayashi Akihito, Hirose Kazuo, and Wada Atsushi, Gakuseisha, 2008
== 論文 ==
* [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E6%B2%B3%E4%B8%8A%E9%82%A6%E5%BD%A6&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=1&type=1 Cinii (Kunihiko Kawakami)]

{{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1945|5|10}}|birth_place={{ill|Osaka|qid=Q35765}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|archaeologist|qid=Q3621491}}|native_name=河上邦彦}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q21699496]] Wikidata name: Kunihiko Kawakami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q21699496]] Wikidata name: Kunihiko Kawakami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox academic | name = Kunihiko Kawakami | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] | death_date = | death_place = | other_names = | main_interests = [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]] | alma_mater = [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] | workplaces = [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] | degree = Doctor of Letters | URL = | years_active = | spouse = | children = }} '''Kunihiko Kawakami''' (河上 邦彦, born [[:en:May 10|May 10]], [[:en:1945|1945]]) is a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 考古学}} and former professor at [[:en:Kobe Yamate University|Kobe Yamate University]].
== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]] in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctor of Letters|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士（文学）}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at [[:en:Peking University|Peking University]]. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at [[:en:Northwest University (China)|Northwest University (China)]], and visiting researcher at the [[:en:Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences]]' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō Town]] Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of [[:en:Asuka, Yamato|Asuka Palace]], [[:en:Bakuya Kofun|Makino Kofun]], {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmyōjin Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurozuka Kofun]].<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>

* *Archaeological Sketches*, Rokkō Shu11an, 1989 * *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*, Yūzankaku Publishing, 1995 * *Excavating Asuka*, Kodansha Sensho Metier, 2003 * *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Sankei News Service, 2004 * *Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Tumuli*, Shinsensha, 2006
=== 共編著 ===
* *Studies on Asuka* (2 volumes), co-edited with {{ill|Wada Atsushi|ja|和田萃|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Atsushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Sugaya Fuminori|ja|菅谷文則|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugaya Fuminori|qq=}}, Jinbun Shoin, 1996 * *Terminal Kofun in Yamato*, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Archaeology of Yamato: Volume 3 - Yamato’s Kofun 2*, overseen by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, Kinki Nippon Railway, 2006 * *Mount Miwa and Ancient Kami Worship*, co-authored with {{ill|Ogasawara Yoshihiko|ja|小笠原好彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Yoshihiko|qq=}}, Sugaya Fuminori, Suzuka Chiyono, Hirabayashi Akihito, Hirose Kazuo, and Wada Atsushi, Gakuseisha, 2008

== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]] in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctor of Letters|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士（文学）|1=Doctor of Letters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q837343}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at [[:en:Peking University|Peking University]]. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at [[:en:Northwest University (China)|Northwest University (China)]], and visiting researcher at the [[:en:Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences]]' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō Town]] Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of [[:en:Asuka, Yamato|Asuka Palace]], [[:en:Bakuya Kofun|Makino Kofun]], {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmyōjin Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurozuka Kofun]].<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>

== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]] in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctor of Letters|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士（文学）|1=Doctor of Letters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q837343}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at [[:en:Peking University|Peking University]]. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at [[:en:Northwest University (China)|Northwest University (China)]], and visiting researcher at the [[:en:Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences]]' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō Town]] Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of [[:en:Asuka, Yamato|Asuka Palace]], [[:en:Bakuya Kofun|Makino Kofun]], {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurozuka Kofun]].<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>

== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]] in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctor of Letters|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士（文学）|1=Doctor of Letters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q837343}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at [[:en:Peking University|Peking University]]. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at [[:en:Northwest University (China)|Northwest University (China)]], and visiting researcher at the [[:en:Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences]]' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō Town]] Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of [[:en:Asuka, Yamato|Asuka Palace]], [[:en:Bakuya Kofun|Makino Kofun]], {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurozuka Kofun]].<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>

== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]] in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctor of Letters|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士（文学）|1=Doctor of Letters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q837343}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at [[:en:Peking University|Peking University]]. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at [[:en:Northwest University (China)|Northwest University (China)]], and visiting researcher at the [[:en:Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences]]' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō Town]] Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of [[:en:Asuka, Yamato|Asuka Palace]], [[:en:Bakuya Kofun|Makino Kofun]], {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurozuka Kofun]].<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:河上邦彦]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:河上邦彦]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:河上邦彦]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1945|5|10}}|birth_place={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|archaeologist|WD=Q3621491}}|native_name=河上邦彦}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q21699496]] Wikidata name: Kunihiko Kawakami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q21699496]] Wikidata name: Kunihiko Kawakami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox academic | name = Kunihiko Kawakami | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] | death_date = | death_place = | other_names = | main_interests = [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]] | alma_mater = [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] | workplaces = [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] | degree = Doctor of Letters | URL = | years_active = | spouse = | children = }} '''Kunihiko Kawakami''' (河上 邦彦, born [[:en:May 10|May 10]], [[:en:1945|1945]]) is a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 考古学|1=archaeology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23498}} and former professor at [[:en:Kobe Yamate University|Kobe Yamate University]].
== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]] in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctor of Letters|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士（文学）|1=Doctor of Letters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q837343}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at [[:en:Peking University|Peking University]]. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at [[:en:Northwest University (China)|Northwest University (China)]], and visiting researcher at the [[:en:Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences]]' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō Town]] Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of [[:en:Asuka, Yamato|Asuka Palace]], [[:en:Bakuya Kofun|Makino Kofun]], {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurozuka Kofun]].<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
== 著書 ==
* *Archaeological Sketches*, Rokkō Shu11an, 1989 * *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*, Yūzankaku Publishing, 1995 * *Excavating Asuka*, Kodansha Sensho Metier, 2003 * *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Sankei News Service, 2004 * *Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Tumuli*, Shinsensha, 2006
=== 共編著 ===
* *Studies on Asuka* (2 volumes), co-edited with {{ill|Wada Atsushi|ja|和田萃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Atsushi|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}} and {{ill|Sugaya Fuminori|ja|菅谷文則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugaya Fuminori|1=Fuminori Sugaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21700046}}, Jinbun Shoin, 1996 * *Terminal Kofun in Yamato*, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Archaeology of Yamato: Volume 3 - Yamato’s Kofun 2*, overseen by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, Kinki Nippon Railway, 2006 * *Mount Miwa and Ancient Kami Worship*, co-authored with {{ill|Ogasawara Yoshihiko|ja|小笠原好彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Yoshihiko|1=Yoshihiko Ogasawara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699383}}, Sugaya Fuminori, Suzuka Chiyono, Hirabayashi Akihito, Hirose Kazuo, and Wada Atsushi, Gakuseisha, 2008

Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|en|Northwest University (China)|WD=Q2496116|lt=Northwest University (China)}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|en|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|WD=Q1073828|lt=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō Town}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka, Yamato|en|Asuka, Yamato|WD=Q752394|lt=Asuka Palace}}, {{ill|Bakuya Kofun|en|Bakuya Kofun|WD=Q11570540|lt=Makino Kofun}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|lt=Kurozuka Kofun}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>

Kunihiko Kawakami was born in {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}} in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at {{ill|Kansai University|en|Kansai University|WD=Q1149393|lt=Kansai University}} with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctor of Letters|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士（文学）|1=Doctor of Letters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q837343}} degree.

From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at {{ill|Peking University|en|Peking University|WD=Q16952|lt=Peking University}}. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|en|Northwest University (China)|WD=Q2496116|lt=Northwest University (China)}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|en|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|WD=Q1073828|lt=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō Town}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka, Yamato|en|Asuka, Yamato|WD=Q752394|lt=Asuka Palace}}, {{ill|Bakuya Kofun|en|Bakuya Kofun|WD=Q11570540|lt=Makino Kofun}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|lt=Kurozuka Kofun}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>

{{Infobox academic | name = Kunihiko Kawakami | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} | death_date = | death_place = | other_names = | main_interests = {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|WD=Q23498|lt=Archaeology|1=Archaeology}} | alma_mater = {{ill|Kansai University|en|Kansai University|WD=Q1149393|lt=Kansai University|1=Kansai University}} | workplaces = {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} | degree = Doctor of Letters | URL = | years_active = | spouse = | children = }} '''Kunihiko Kawakami''' (河上 邦彦, born {{ill|May 10|en|May 10|WD=Q2557|lt=May 10|1=May 10}}, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}}) is a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 考古学|1=Archaeology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23498}} and former professor at {{ill|Kobe Yamate University|en|Kobe Yamate University|WD=Q6424350|lt=Kobe Yamate University|1=Kobe Yamate University}}.
== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}} in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at {{ill|Kansai University|en|Kansai University|WD=Q1149393|lt=Kansai University|1=Kansai University}} with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctor of Letters|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士（文学）|1=Doctor of Letters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q837343}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at {{ill|Peking University|en|Peking University|WD=Q16952|lt=Peking University|1=Peking University}}. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|en|Northwest University (China)|WD=Q2496116|lt=Northwest University (China)|1=Northwest University (China)}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|en|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|WD=Q1073828|lt=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|1=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō Town|1=Kōryō, Nara}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka, Yamato|en|Asuka, Yamato|WD=Q752394|lt=Asuka Palace|1=Asuka, Yamato}}, {{ill|Bakuya Kofun|en|Bakuya Kofun|WD=Q11570540|lt=Makino Kofun|1=Bakuya Kofun}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|lt=Kurozuka Kofun|1=Kurozuka Kofun}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
== 著書 ==
* *Archaeological Sketches*, Rokkō Shu11an, 1989 * *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*, Yūzankaku Publishing, 1995 * *Excavating Asuka*, Kodansha Sensho Metier, 2003 * *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Sankei News Service, 2004 * *Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Tumuli*, Shinsensha, 2006
=== 共編著 ===
* *Studies on Asuka* (2 volumes), co-edited with {{ill|Wada Atsushi|ja|和田萃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Atsushi|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}} and {{ill|Sugaya Fuminori|ja|菅谷文則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugaya Fuminori|1=Fuminori Sugaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21700046}}, Jinbun Shoin, 1996 * *Terminal Kofun in Yamato*, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Archaeology of Yamato: Volume 3 - Yamato’s Kofun 2*, overseen by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, Kinki Nippon Railway, 2006 * *Mount Miwa and Ancient Kami Worship*, co-authored with {{ill|Ogasawara Yoshihiko|ja|小笠原好彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Yoshihiko|1=Yoshihiko Ogasawara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699383}}, Sugaya Fuminori, Suzuka Chiyono, Hirabayashi Akihito, Hirose Kazuo, and Wada Atsushi, Gakuseisha, 2008
== 論文 ==
* [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E6%B2%B3%E4%B8%8A%E9%82%A6%E5%BD%A6&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=1&type=1 Cinii (Kunihiko Kawakami)]

Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|en|Northwest University (China)|qid=Q2496116|lt=Northwest University (China)|1=Northwest University (China)}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|en|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|qid=Q1073828|lt=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|1=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|qid=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō Town|1=Kōryō, Nara}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka, Yamato|en|Asuka, Yamato|qid=Q752394|lt=Asuka Palace|1=Asuka, Yamato}}, {{ill|Bakuya Kofun|en|Bakuya Kofun|qid=Q11570540|lt=Makino Kofun|1=Bakuya Kofun}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|qid=Q1793171|lt=Kurozuka Kofun|1=Kurozuka Kofun}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>
== 著書 ==
* *Archaeological Sketches*, Rokkō Shu11an, 1989 * *Research on Late and Terminal Kofun Period Tumuli*, Yūzankaku Publishing, 1995 * *Excavating Asuka*, Kodansha Sensho Metier, 2003 * *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Sankei News Service, 2004 * *Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Tumuli*, Shinsensha, 2006
=== 共編著 ===
* *Studies on Asuka* (2 volumes), co-edited with {{ill|Wada Atsushi|ja|和田萃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Atsushi|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11418120}} and {{ill|Sugaya Fuminori|ja|菅谷文則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugaya Fuminori|1=Fuminori Sugaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21700046}}, Jinbun Shoin, 1996 * *Terminal Kofun in Yamato*, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Archaeology of Yamato: Volume 3 - Yamato’s Kofun 2*, overseen by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, Kinki Nippon Railway, 2006 * *Mount Miwa and Ancient Kami Worship*, co-authored with {{ill|Ogasawara Yoshihiko|ja|小笠原好彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Yoshihiko|1=Yoshihiko Ogasawara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21699383}}, Sugaya Fuminori, Suzuka Chiyono, Hirabayashi Akihito, Hirose Kazuo, and Wada Atsushi, Gakuseisha, 2008
== 論文 ==

{{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1945|5|10}}|birth_place={{ill|Osaka|qid=Q35765|1=Osaka}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|archaeologist|qid=Q3621491|1=Archaeologist}}|native_name=河上邦彦}}
{{Infobox academic | name = Kunihiko Kawakami | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1945|5|10}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} | death_date = | death_place = | other_names = | main_interests = {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|qid=Q23498|lt=Archaeology|1=Archaeology}} | alma_mater = {{ill|Kansai University|en|Kansai University|qid=Q1149393|lt=Kansai University|1=Kansai University}} | workplaces = {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|qid=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} | degree = Doctor of Letters | URL = | years_active = | spouse = | children = }} '''Kunihiko Kawakami''' (河上 邦彦, born {{ill|May 10|en|May 10|qid=Q2557|lt=May 10|1=May 10}}, {{ill|1945|en|1945|qid=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}}) is a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 考古学|1=Archaeology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q23498}} and former professor at {{ill|Kobe Yamate University|en|Kobe Yamate University|qid=Q6424350|lt=Kobe Yamate University|1=Kobe Yamate University}}.
== 経歴 ==
Kunihiko Kawakami was born in {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|qid=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}} in 1945. He completed his graduate studies at {{ill|Kansai University|en|Kansai University|qid=Q1149393|lt=Kansai University|1=Kansai University}} with a master’s degree in Japanese history in 1965. In 1995, he submitted his dissertation *Research on Late Kofun Period Tumuli* to Kansai University and was awarded a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|ja|文学博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctor of Letters|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士（文学）|1=Doctor of Letters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q837343}} degree.
After graduation, he began working at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|qid=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} in 1970 and became a researcher there in 1972. From 1980 to 1982, he studied abroad at {{ill|Peking University|en|Peking University|qid=Q16952|lt=Peking University|1=Peking University}}. He later held positions as Director of the Third Survey Division, Deputy Director of the institute, and Director of its affiliated museum. Additionally, he served as a professor at Kobe Yamate University, guest professor at {{ill|Northwest University (China)|en|Northwest University (China)|qid=Q2496116|lt=Northwest University (China)|1=Northwest University (China)}}, and visiting researcher at the {{ill|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|en|Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|qid=Q1073828|lt=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences|1=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences}}' Ancient Civilization Research Center. He was also the Director of the Cultural Properties Preservation Center for the {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|qid=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō Town|1=Kōryō, Nara}} Board of Education.
He participated in numerous archaeological surveys, including the sites of {{ill|Asuka, Yamato|en|Asuka, Yamato|qid=Q752394|lt=Asuka Palace|1=Asuka, Yamato}}, {{ill|Bakuya Kofun|en|Bakuya Kofun|qid=Q11570540|lt=Makino Kofun|1=Bakuya Kofun}}, {{ill|Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja|東明神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmyōjin Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|qid=Q1793171|lt=Kurozuka Kofun|1=Kurozuka Kofun}}.<ref>『大和葛城の大古墳群』著者紹介</ref>

=== 共編著 ===
